message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Anime Tactical Simulator Raid Carries: Complete Guide & Strategies 2026",
  description: "Master anime tactical simulator raid carries with our 2026 guide. Learn unit requirements, evolution paths, and how to farm Stat Cubes and Medals efficiently.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Raid+Carries+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Anime Tactical Simulator Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Raid Entry**: You should have at least 3-4 Mythic units at level 50+ before attempting **anime tactical simulator raid carries**.
- **Core Rewards**: Farm raids primarily for **Stat Cubes** to upgrade talents and **Medals** for exclusive item exchanges.
- **Unit Management**: Always keep Secret and Mythic units; never sell them as they are required for late-game evolution.
- **Slot Priority**: Use Tower Mode to unlock additional unit slots, which significantly increases your total damage output during raids.
- **Community Tip**: Join the official Discord to find high-level players for coordinated raid runs and faster material farming.
</Callout>

Mastering the mechanics of **anime tactical simulator raid carries** is the ultimate goal for any player looking to dominate the leaderboards in 2026. Raids represent the pinnacle of cooperative tactical gameplay, requiring a blend of high-tier unit placement, precise ability timing, and deep knowledge of unit evolution. Whether you are a solo player looking to build a team that can carry others or a beginner seeking to understand how to contribute to a high-level squad, understanding the synergy between Mythic units and Stat Cube progression is essential.

In this comprehensive guide, we will break down the exact requirements for entering raids, the best units to prioritize for maximum damage, and the step-by-step progression needed to move from World 1 basics to end-game raid dominance. By utilizing the latest 2026 strategies, including optimal Tower Mode climbing and talent tree optimization, you can transform your squad into a powerhouse capable of clearing the toughest content the game has to offer.

<Section icon={Map} title="Raid Entry Requirements and Preparation">

Before you can effectively engage in **anime tactical simulator raid carries**, you must ensure your account meets the minimum power thresholds. Entering raids too early is a common mistake that leads to wasted time and resources. The enemies in raid modes have significantly higher HP pools than those found in standard World stages, necessitating a team that can sustain high DPS over long periods.

<Callout type="warning" title="Minimum Threshold Warning">
Do not attempt raids until you have at least 3-4 Mythic units. Attempting raids with only Legendary or Epic units will result in inefficient clears and a lack of meaningful rewards.
</Callout>

To prepare for your first successful raid, focus on the following benchmarks:

| Requirement | Minimum Target | Importance |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Unit Rarity** | 3-4 Mythic Units | Critical |
| **Unit Level** | Level 50+ on main DPS | High |
| **Equip Slots** | 4+ Slots unlocked | Critical |
| **World Progress** | World 2 Completed | Medium |
| **Tower Floor** | Floor 25+ | High |

Building a balanced team is just as important as having high rarity. You need a mix of high-damage carries and utility units that can slow or stun enemies. In the current 2026 meta, the most successful raid teams prioritize units that offer area-of-effect (AoE) damage to handle the dense waves of enemies that precede the raid boss.

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Elite Unit Management: Carries and Support">

In Anime Tactical Simulator, not all units are created equal. To facilitate **anime tactical simulator raid carries**, you must know which units to invest your precious Gems and Souls into. The game uses a hierarchy where Mythic and Secret units reign supreme, especially when evolved.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="Secret Units">
    - **Highest Base Stats**
    - Extremely rare drops
    - Essential for end-game carries
  </Card>
  <Card title="Mythic Units">
    - **Core Damage Dealers**
    - Accessible via summons
    - Required for evolution paths
  </Card>
  <Card title="Legendary Units">
    - **Early Game Baseline**
    - Good for World 1-2
    - Eventually used as fusion fodder
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

Managing your inventory is a skill in itself. Using the "Keep, Fuse, or Sell" strategy ensures you never run out of the resources needed for upgrades.

| Rarity | Primary Action | Reason |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Secret/Mythic** | **KEEP** | Evolution and late-game raid content |
| **Legendary** | **KEEP/FUSE** | Early core damage or high-tier fodder |
| **Rare/Epic** | **FUSE** | Level up your main Mythic units |
| **Common** | **SELL** | Obtain Souls for unit evolution |

<Callout type="tip" title="Evolution Strategy">
Evolution requires duplicate copies of the same unit and specific Souls. Before selling your extra Mythics, check the evolution building to see if you can upgrade your primary carry instead.
</Callout>

According to the [Anime Tactical Simulator Wiki](https://www.animetacticalsimulator.wiki/), selling higher-rarity units in the late game is the only reliable way to obtain the high-tier Souls required for final-stage evolutions. This makes every summon valuable, even if you don't pull a new unit.

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Step-by-Step Progression to Raid Readiness">

Reaching the level where you can provide **anime tactical simulator raid carries** requires a structured approach to progression. You cannot jump straight into the hardest content; you must build a foundation through World stages and Tower Mode.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Redeem Active Codes">
    Immediately use codes like `RELEASE` and `UPDATE1` to secure Gems and Dungeon Keys. This provides the initial boost needed to pull your first Mythic units.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Clear World 1 & 2">
    Focus on finishing basic kill quests and unlocking World 2. Avoid grinding bosses in World 1, as the time-to-reward ratio is poor compared to later stages.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Push Tower Mode Milestones">
    Climb to at least Floor 50 in Tower Mode. This is critical because it unlocks additional unit deployment slots, which is the single biggest multiplier for your total raid damage.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Farm Stat Cubes in Raids">
    Once your team is stable, enter raids to farm Stat Cubes. Use these to upgrade your Talent trees, which provide permanent, account-wide stat boosts.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Callout type="info" title="Community Resources">
If you are struggling to clear a specific raid, the [Official Discord](http://animetactical.wiki/) is the best place to find experienced players who can assist with carries and strategy coordination.
</Callout>

World 3 introduces a massive jump in enemy HP. If you find your damage stalling, return to World 2 or the Tower to optimize your traits and talents before pushing further.

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Maximizing Raid Rewards and Talents">

The primary reason to engage in **anime tactical simulator raid carries** is the exclusive loot. Raids are the only source for certain high-level materials that are required to push your units to their absolute limit.

| Reward | Primary Use | How to Optimize |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Stat Cubes** | **Talent Upgrades** | Farm high-difficulty raids for better drop rates |
| **Medals** | **Item Exchange** | Save for exclusive evolution stones or tokens |
| **Rare Materials** | **Unit Crafting** | Focus on specific raids that drop the mats you need |
| **Gems** | **Summoning** | Use for limited-time banners with S-tier units |

<Callout type="success" title="Talent Tree Priority">
Always prioritize "Damage %" and "Attack Speed" in your talent tree first. These stats have the highest impact on your ability to clear waves efficiently during raids.
</Callout>

Talents are permanent upgrades. Unlike traits, which are unit-specific and can be rerolled, talents apply to your entire squad. This makes raiding a mandatory part of long-term account growth.

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Tower Mode: The Secret to Raid Damage">

While raids provide the materials for power, Tower Mode provides the capacity for power. Many players focus solely on unit levels and forget that the number of units you can deploy is determined by your Tower progress. To be a successful provider of **anime tactical simulator raid carries**, you must unlock as many slots as possible.

<Checklist
  id="tower-milestones"
  title="Critical Tower Floor Goals:"
  items={[
    "Floor 10: Unlocks +1 Unit Slot (Essential)",
    "Floor 25: Unlocks +1 Unit Slot (High Priority)",
    "Floor 50: Grants Rare Items and +1 Unit Slot",
    "Floor 100: Grants Secret Unit and +1 Unit Slot (End-game Goal)"
  ]}
/>

<Callout type="tip" title="Performance Optimization">
If you experience lag during high-floor Tower runs or intense raids, disable ability VFX and raid effects in the game settings. This can significantly improve your FPS and prevent placement errors.
</Callout>

| Tower Milestone | Reward Type | Strategy |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Floor 10** | **Deployment Slot** | Use basic Mythics to clear easily |
| **Floor 50** | **Rare Materials** | Requires level 60+ units and evolved forms |
| **Floor 75** | **Exclusive Unit** | Focus on AoE units for large waves |
| **Floor 100** | **Secret Unit** | The ultimate test of your raid carry potential |

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "How do I start providing anime tactical simulator raid carries for others?",
    answer: "To carry others, you generally need a full team of evolved Mythic or Secret units, all unit slots unlocked from Tower Floor 100, and highly invested Talent trees (Level 50+). You should also have optimized traits like 'Godly' or 'Unique' on your main DPS units."
  },
  {
    question: "What is the best way to get Stat Cubes quickly?",
    answer: "The fastest way to get Stat Cubes is by clearing high-difficulty raids. If you cannot clear them solo, join a raid group in the official Discord where a high-level player can carry the team, ensuring everyone gets the rewards."
  },
  {
    question: "Should I spend Gems on trait rerolls or summons?",
    answer: "In the early and mid-game, always prioritize summons to build a full Mythic roster. Only spend Gems or Premium Rerolls on traits once you have your 'forever' units that you plan to evolve and use in end-game raids."
  },
  {
    question: "Why am I not doing enough damage in World 3 raids?",
    answer: "World 3 features a massive HP jump. Ensure you have evolved your core units and that you are utilizing the maximum number of deployment slots from Tower Mode. Also, check if your unit types match the enemy weaknesses."
  }
]} />

<Callout type="info" title="Final Summary">
Success in **anime tactical simulator raid carries** comes down to three things: unlocking deployment slots in the Tower, farming Stat Cubes for account-wide Talents, and strategically evolving your Mythic units. Stay updated with the latest 2026 codes to keep your resource bank full!
</Callout>

</Section>
